meta.title: Ulta AI CLI — Skill Call Graph & Workflows
meta.description: Who calls whom, the canonical workflows, and when each skill fires

props.title: Skill Call Graph & Workflows
props.subtitle: 33 skills, 4 lanes, 6 canonical workflows — extracted live from src/skills/ after the Tier 0 fixes

props.content: Arrows mean a SKILL.md explicitly routes to or requires another skill. Skills with no inbound arrows are not dead — agents self-route by scanning frontmatter descriptions per task, so a precise trigger phrase IS the wiring.

props.title: 1. Hardcoded pipeline
props.content: Only review-checklist and review-gates are wired into code: src/lib/review/bundle.js injects them into every PR review. Everything else is soft-routed.

props.title: 2. Cross-skill chaining
props.content: A skill's body names the next skill (REQUIRED SUB-SKILL or 'then use'). This forms the workflows below — e.g. brainstorming requires writing-plans; systematic-debugging routes to root-cause-tracing.

props.title: 3. Description self-routing
props.content: using-ulta mandates scanning all skill descriptions each task. On-demand skills (dep-audit, weekly-status, promoting-skills) are reached purely by trigger-phrase match — by design.

props.label: LANE 1
props.title: Core delivery flow
props.subtitle: The spine: every feature follows this chain; dotted edges are cross-cutting helpers

props.content:
graph LR UU[using-ulta
session start] --> BR[brainstorming] BR <--> RC[research-conductor] BR --> WP[writing-plans] WP --> EP[executing-plans] WP --> SDD[subagent-driven-development] GW[using-git-worktrees] -.isolation.-> EP GW -.isolation.-> SDD TDD[test-driven-development] -.per task.-> EP TDD -.per task.-> SDD EP --> RG[review-gates] SDD --> RG RG --> FB[finishing-a-development-branch] FB -.cleanup.-> GW

props.label: LANE 2
props.title: Debugging lane
props.subtitle: Entered from ANY stage the moment a bug or failing test appears; exits back into the delivery flow

props.content:
graph LR SD[systematic-debugging
any bug or test failure] --> RCT[root-cause-tracing] RCT --> DD[defense-in-depth] SD --> CBW[condition-based-waiting
flaky timing tests] SD --> TDD[test-driven-development
regression test first] SD --> VBC[verification-before-completion] TAP[testing-anti-patterns] -.guards mocks.-> TDD

props.label: LANE 3
props.title: Review & standards web
props.subtitle: review-checklist is the hub — the most-referenced skill in the system; thick edges are the hardcoded PR pipeline

props.content:
graph TD PR[PR pipeline
bundle.js hardcodes these] ==> RC2[review-checklist] PR ==> RG2[review-gates] RA[review-audit
batch audits] --> RC2 RA --> RG2 SC[standards-code] --> RC2 ST[standards-testing] --> RC2 SCM[standards-commit] --> RC2 SDOC[standards-documentation] --> RC2 RAU[repo-automation
hooks + CI setup] --> SC RAU --> ST RAU --> SCM REQ[requesting-code-review] --> RC2 REC[receiving-code-review] --> RC2 RG2 --> VBC2[verification-before-completion]

props.label: LANES 4 + 5
props.title: Onboarding & skill governance
props.subtitle: Run once per repo (onboarding) and whenever a repo-local skill graduates (governance)

props.content:
graph LR RO[repo-onboarding
ulta init / doctor / repair] --> RCX[repo-context
01-business 02-architecture 03-delivery] RO --> RR[repo-rules
.ulta/rules: style testing review gotchas] RCX <--> RR WS[writing-skills
author + test skills] --> PS[promoting-skills
ulta skill promote -> catalog PR]

props.label: CANONICAL WORKFLOWS
props.title: The six actual workflows
props.subtitle: When you'd run each chain, end to end

props.title: 🚀 W1 — Feature delivery (the default)
props.content: **Trigger:** any new feature or multi-step change. **Chain:** using-ulta → brainstorming (↔ research-conductor for pattern/LOE scans) → writing-plans → executing-plans (batch + human checkpoints) OR subagent-driven-development (fresh subagent per task, two-stage review) → review-gates → finishing-a-development-branch. using-git-worktrees isolates the work; test-driven-development governs every task inside implementation.

props.title: 🐛 W2 — Bug fix / debugging
props.content: **Trigger:** any bug, failing test, or unexpected behavior — BEFORE proposing a fix. **Chain:** systematic-debugging (4-phase root-cause framework) → root-cause-tracing (trace back through the stack) → defense-in-depth (validate at every layer) → test-driven-development (regression test first) → verification-before-completion. condition-based-waiting if the failure is flaky/timing; testing-anti-patterns when touching mocks.

props.title: 🔍 W3 — PR review (pipeline-hardcoded)
props.content: **Trigger:** every pull request — automatic. **Chain:** bundle.js injects review-checklist + review-gates into the PR prompt → standards-code/testing/commit/documentation overlay repo-specific rules from .ulta/rules → verification-before-completion before any approval claim. Humans use requesting-code-review (sender) and receiving-code-review (recipient rigor).

props.title: 📦 W4 — Repo onboarding
props.content: **Trigger:** installing, updating, or repairing an Ulta-enabled repo. **Chain:** repo-onboarding (ulta init → doctor → additive repair) → repo-context (generates 01-business-context, 02-architecture, 03-delivery-context) → repo-rules (extracts code-style/testing/review/gotchas into .ulta/rules from real repo evidence). This is what makes every other skill repo-aware — context first, grep later.

props.title: 🧬 W5 — Skill governance
props.content: **Trigger:** creating or refining a skill. **Chain:** writing-skills (TDD for process docs — test with subagents before writing) → iterate repo-locally in .agents/skills/ → promoting-skills (ulta skill promote → structured ULTA_PROMOTE_* errors → Bitbucket PR to the shared catalog).

props.title: ⏱ W6 — On-demand & reporting
props.content: **Trigger:** explicit ask or schedule; no inbound arrows by design. dep-audit (audit project dependencies), weekly-status (end-of-week report from commits/handoffs — currently needs a real invoker), repo-automation (set up hooks + CI, routes into the standards skills), gemini-agentic-cli (headless CLI automation in CI/CD).

props.label: REFERENCE
props.title: Every skill: when it fires, what it references, who references it
props.subtitle: 'When' is the live frontmatter trigger agents match against. 'None (self-routed)' = reached by description match only.

rows.when: Use when starting any conversation - establishes mandatory workflows for finding and using skills, introduces ulta CLI commands, and defines the skill-driven development workflow
